dmTitle.techName: ECS Monitoring, Diagnostics and Control Interfaces

displayText.simplePara: AMPEL360e Wide Tube-and-Wing Family (eWTW) — All variants

remarks.simplePara: Programme-controlled extension (ATA 21-80). Monitoring and diagnostics integration beyond standard ATA chapter 21 scope. infoCode 040A subject to BREX/SNS/DMRL freeze. Not for operational use.

levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module describes the ECS Monitoring, Diagnostics and Control Interfaces of the AMPEL360e eWTW aircraft, aligned to ATLAS node 021-080 and programme-extended ATA section 21-80. This is a programme-controlled extension that consolidates the health monitoring, fault detection, prognostics and crew/maintenance interface functions of the ECS into a single descriptive data module.

levelledPara.title: ECS Health Monitoring Architecture
levelledPara.para: The eWTW ECS monitoring system is integrated with the aircraft Central Maintenance System (CMS) and the Prognostics and Health Management (PHM) system. The monitoring architecture covers:

listItem.para: Pack and compression subsystem health: pack inlet/outlet conditions, valve positions, compressor speeds (electric variants), fault detection.
Pressurization system health: cabin differential pressure, outflow valve position, controller status, safety valve status.
Temperature control health: zone temperatures, trim air valve positions, duct overheat status.
Air quality monitoring: particulate sensors (optional), CO and ozone sensors (applicable variants).
Filter condition monitoring: differential pressure across HEPA filters for replacement interval determination.

levelledPara.title: Crew Interface
levelledPara.para: The ECS presents system status and crew alerting through the following crew interface elements:

listItem.para: ECAM (Electronic Centralised Aircraft Monitor) or equivalent aircraft monitoring system: ECS page displaying pack status, cabin temperature, pressure differential and outflow valve position.
Overhead panel controls: pack switches, temperature selectors, pressurization mode and target selectors.
Warning and caution alerts: defined by the crew alerting system for ECS faults (e.g., PACK FAULT, DUCT OVERHEAT, DIFF PRESS LO).
Fault codes and fault history accessible via the CMS maintenance terminal.
Built-In Test Equipment (BITE) for line and base maintenance troubleshooting.
Prognostic data outputs for filter replacement, valve wear and compressor health trending.
Data download interface for ECS operational data (digital thread integration).

levelledPara.title: Digital Thread and PHM Integration
levelledPara.para: ECS health monitoring data is part of the eWTW digital thread. Sensor data, fault records and prognostic indicators are fed to the aircraft PHM system and can be transmitted to the ground via ACARS or equivalent datalink for real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance scheduling. This integration supports the CSDB traceability framework described in DMC-AMPEL360E-EWTW-021-090-00A-040A-D.
